title: Unit vs integration tests
prompt_md:
You have two tests for an order service. Test A calls a pure `calculate_total()` function with hard-coded inputs and no I/O. Test B spins up a real Postgres database, inserts an order, and reads it back through the repository layer.

Which test is the **integration test** (the one that crosses a real boundary like a database)?
options: Test B, because it exercises multiple components together and hits a real database
Both are unit tests; integration tests only run in CI
Neither — touching a database makes it an end-to-end test, not integration
Test A, because it tests application logic
explanation_md: Test B is the integration test: it crosses a real boundary (a live database) and verifies several components working together. Test A is a classic unit test — one function, no I/O, fast and isolated. Integration tests are slower but catch wiring/serialization/SQL bugs that unit tests can't see.
